name: Security Architecture
lever_classification: governance — who maintains physical and digital access controls
consequences: Implementing biometric access controls directly links personnel accountability to facility integrity. This risks fostering a culture of suspicion, but could reduce unauthorized resource diversion. A downstream implication is potential resistance from residents accustomed to privacy.
options: Deploy biometric scanners at all internal checkpoints
Use decentralized human guard teams with randomized patrol routes
Introduce voluntary neural-implant monitoring for high-risk zones
review: Centralized biometric systems create single points of failure; none of the options address potential system-wide breaches from compromised authentication data.

name: Resource Allocation Model
lever_classification: operations — how to distribute spatial and material resources
consequences: Prioritizing agricultural output over industrial production accelerates food security but delays infrastructure completion. This creates a temporary reliance on external supply chains for critical machinery parts.
options: Allocate 60% of floor space to hydroponic farms
Distribute resource quotas via blockchain-verified smart contracts
Establish a temporary surface-level mining operation for raw materials
review: The options assume linear resource needs; none account for cyclical demand shifts during construction phases.

name: Information Control Protocol
lever_classification: governance — what information is accessible to residents
consequences: Restricting external media access maintains ideological cohesion but increases risk of internal misinformation. This could lead to unregulated knowledge sharing through informal networks.
options: Broadcast curated news summaries through public terminals
Require all communication devices to use government-approved content filters
Implement peer-to-peer information audits with random verification checks
review: The options focus on suppression rather than education; none address long-term cognitive resilience against information scarcity.

name: Emergency Evacuation Strategy
lever_classification: operations — how to manage crisis response protocols
consequences: Designing vertical escape tunnels increases structural complexity but provides rapid exit routes. This could compromise floor stability during seismic events.
options: Construct reinforced emergency shafts every 12 floors
Equip all residents with pressurized escape capsules
Develop subterranean transit corridors for mass evacuation
review: The options assume uniform disaster scenarios; none account for localized failures in the 144-floor hierarchy.

name: Labor Organization Framework
lever_classification: execution — how to structure workforce deployment
consequences: Implementing shift-based work cycles increases productivity but risks chronic fatigue among workers. This could lead to reduced oversight of critical systems.
options: Rotate crews through 18-hour work blocks with mandatory rest periods
Introduce AI-driven task optimization for manual labor
Create hybrid human-machine maintenance teams
review: The options focus on efficiency metrics; none address psychological impacts of prolonged underground confinement.

name: Societal Cohesion Protocol
lever_classification: governance — how to maintain social order under extreme control
consequences: Centralizing decision-making accelerates implementation but risks bureaucratic inertia during crises. Decentralized authority models improve adaptability but may erode compliance with core directives.
options: Implement hierarchical social stratification to enforce compliance
Establish peer monitoring networks to self-regulate behavior
Introduce rotational leadership cycles to prevent power consolidation
review: Maintaining order through enforced hierarchy risks internal rebellion; none of the options address psychological decay from prolonged isolation.

name: Environmental Regulation Matrix
lever_classification: operations — how to manage self-sustaining environmental systems
consequences: Automated ecosystem management reduces human error but creates single points of failure. Manual oversight ensures resilience but slows response times during system malfunctions.
options: Deploy closed-loop bioreactors for waste-to-resource conversion
Integrate geothermal energy with solar arrays for redundancy
Adopt modular ecosystem zones with adaptive climate controls
review: Over-reliance on automated systems creates vulnerability; the options ignore cascading failures from interdependent subsystems.

name: Behavioral Monitoring System
lever_classification: execution — how to enforce behavioral conformity
consequences: Real-time surveillance prevents dissent but triggers psychological distress. Anonymized data analysis preserves morale but reduces actionable insights.
options: Use neural feedback implants to detect dissent
Install AI-driven sentiment analysis in public spaces
Require mandatory psychological evaluations for all residents
review: Surveillance-driven compliance creates a culture of fear; the options fail to account for long-term mental health deterioration.

name: Resource Circulation Strategy
lever_classification: operations — how to manage material and energy flows
consequences: Prioritizing recycling maximizes efficiency but limits scalability. External resupply ensures flexibility but introduces dependency risks.
options: Create zero-waste material loops through on-site processing
Maintain stockpiles of critical supplies in sealed underground vaults
Establish periodic surface extraction missions for rare resources
review: Stockpiling creates storage inefficiencies; none of the options address the logistical challenges of surface operations.

name: Investor Alignment Framework
lever_classification: governance — how to balance stakeholder interests
consequences: Direct stakeholder involvement ensures funding stability but introduces conflicting priorities. Autonomous governance preserves vision but risks financial instability.
options: Form a governing council with equal private and public sector representation
Tie executive compensation to long-term system resilience metrics
Implement quarterly transparency audits for all funding sources
review: Shared governance creates decision-making bottlenecks; the options overlook potential sabotage from disgruntled investors.

name: Cognitive Isolation Framework
lever_classification: methodology — how to manage information exposure
consequences: Restricting external information prevents ideological contamination but stifles innovation. Controlled information exposure fosters creativity but increases subversion risks.
options: Block all external communication through signal jamming technology
Curate a restricted digital archive of approved historical data
Require mandatory indoctrination sessions for new arrivals
review: Total information control creates knowledge stagnation; the options ignore the risk of internal misinformation campaigns.

name: Crisis Response Protocol
lever_classification: execution — how to manage emergency scenarios
consequences: Predefined emergency procedures ensure rapid action but lack flexibility. Decentralized response teams improve adaptability but require extensive training.
options: Establish centralized command centers with pre-approved escalation protocols
Train cross-functional resident brigades for multi-domain emergencies
Develop AI-driven scenario simulations for real-time decision support
review: Centralized command structures create single points of failure; the options don't address communication breakdowns during power outages.

name: Energy Resilience Framework
consequences: Prioritizing nuclear fission reactors accelerates energy output but increases risk of catastrophic failure. Solar thermal arrays reduce dependency on finite fuels but limit power during prolonged maintenance. Geothermal exchange networks provide stable output but require extensive subterranean excavation. The choice directly affects operational continuity and emergency preparedness.
options: Deploy modular nuclear fission reactors with automated cooling systems
Install solar thermal arrays integrated into structural walls for passive energy capture
Construct geothermal exchange networks tapping subterranean heat sources
review: Balancing immediate energy needs against long-term safety risks creates a core tension. None of the options address potential geological shifts disrupting geothermal infrastructure.

name: Waste Reintegration Protocol
consequences: Composting organic waste accelerates nutrient cycles but requires dedicated biological processing zones. Incineration reduces volume but emits byproducts needing filtration. Pyrolysis converts waste to biochar but demands high-temperature infrastructure. Each approach impacts air quality and agricultural productivity.
options: Implement anaerobic digesters for organic waste to produce biogas and fertilizer
Establish high-temperature incineration units with particulate scrubbing systems
Deploy pyrolysis chambers to convert non-organic waste into stable carbon compounds
review: Optimizing waste processing efficiency vs. environmental impact creates tension. The options overlook potential microbial resistance in closed-loop systems.

name: Vertical Mobility Network
consequences: Cable-driven elevators enable rapid transit but require frequent mechanical maintenance. Pneumatic tube systems offer high-speed transport but risk pressure imbalances. Gravity-assisted rail systems reduce energy use but limit acceleration. These choices affect worker productivity and emergency response times.
options: Construct high-speed cable elevators with redundant braking mechanisms
Install pressurized pneumatic tubes for cargo and personnel transport
Design gravity-powered rail corridors connecting all floor levels
review: Prioritizing speed vs. reliability creates tension. None of the options account for seismic activity disrupting vertical transit systems.

name: Social Stratification Mechanism
consequences: Merit-based role assignments increase efficiency but risk social unrest. Hereditary class divisions ensure stability but stifle innovation. Rotational assignment systems promote adaptability but reduce expertise. These approaches shape labor dynamics and governance structures.
options: Implement performance-scored job placement with periodic reevaluation
Establish hereditary occupational castes with strict lineage verification
Rotate personnel through multiple roles every 18 months
review: Maintaining order vs. fostering innovation creates tension. The options fail to address potential sabotage from disenfranchised groups.

name: Atmospheric Stewardship System
consequences: Oxygen generators using electrolysis ensure consistent output but consume water reserves. Algae bioreactors purify air naturally but require light exposure. Carbon scrubbers remove CO2 but need frequent maintenance. These choices directly affect air quality and resource consumption.
options: Deploy electrolysis units with closed-loop water recovery systems
Install algae-filled photobioreactors in designated ventilation zones
Construct carbon capture arrays using amine-based absorption techniques
review: Ensuring breathable air vs. conserving resources creates tension. None of the options account for algal bloom risks in enclosed spaces.

name: Material Reclamation Process
consequences: Mechanical sorting facilities maximize recovery rates but require constant staffing. Chemical dissolution tanks extract pure materials but generate hazardous waste. Laser-based separation systems reduce contamination but demand high energy. These methods impact recycling efficiency and safety protocols.
options: Establish automated sorting lines with AI-driven material identification
Build chemical dissolution units for polymer and metal recovery
Implement laser ablation systems for precise material separation
review: Maximizing resource recovery vs. minimizing waste creation creates tension. The options neglect potential chemical runoff from dissolution processes.

name: Psychological Resilience Framework
consequences: Cognitive behavioral conditioning programs reduce dissent but erode individuality. Artistic expression zones improve morale but risk subversive messaging. Social hierarchy reinforcement maintains order but increases stress. These approaches directly affect mental health and compliance rates.
options: Conduct mandatory psychological conditioning sessions with neurofeedback training
Designate communal spaces for creative expression and cultural activities
Enforce strict social hierarchy protocols with status-based privileges
review: Maintaining compliance vs. preserving mental well-being creates tension. None of the options address potential mass psychological breakdown under prolonged isolation.
